metadata.title: Recent Regulatory Updates & Guidance Changes
metadata.purpose: Tracks recently published or finalized regulatory guidance documents that may affect existing protocol designs. Used by the Compliance Watchdog feature to detect compliance drift.

updates.title: Use of Real-World Data in Clinical Studies for Regulatory Decision-Making
updates.summary: First ICH guideline on Real-World Data (RWD) and Real-World Evidence (RWE). Establishes fitness-for-purpose framework for using RWD in regulatory submissions. Covers external control arms, safety surveillance, and post-marketing studies.
impact_on_protocols: Protocols using external/historical controls must now demonstrate RWD fitness-for-purpose per M14 criteria
Safety sections should reference M14 for post-marketing RWE commitments
Protocols with RWE components need data quality assessment plans
Hybrid designs combining RCT + RWE need explicit justification per M14 framework

updates.title: Good Clinical Practice — Modernized Risk-Based Framework
updates.summary: Complete overhaul of GCP requirements. Introduces proportionate risk-based quality management, technology-neutral approach supporting decentralized trials, enhanced data governance requirements, and expanded sponsor oversight obligations.
impact_on_protocols: All protocols must implement risk-based quality management (RBQM) approach
Informed consent sections need updating for electronic/remote consent options
Data integrity section must address source data verification strategies
Monitoring plan must be risk-proportionate, not one-size-fits-all
Protocols must document critical-to-quality factors

updates.title: Adaptive Clinical Trials
updates.summary: First dedicated ICH guideline on adaptive clinical trial designs. Formalizes requirements for interim analyses, sample size re-estimation, treatment arm dropping, Bayesian designs, and type I error control in adaptive settings.
impact_on_protocols: Protocols using adaptive designs must cite E20 and follow its framework
Interim analysis plans need explicit type I error control documentation
Bayesian adaptive designs need prior specification justification per E20
DMC charter must address information access boundaries for adaptive decisions

updates.title: Patient Preference Studies in Clinical Development
updates.summary: New guideline on incorporating patient preference information (PPI) into benefit-risk assessments. Describes when and how to conduct patient preference studies and how regulators will consider PPI in decision-making.
impact_on_protocols: Protocols should consider including patient preference endpoints
Benefit-risk sections should reference patient preference data if available
PRO endpoint selection should align with patient-identified priorities

updates.title: Clinical Electronic Structured Harmonised Protocol (CeSHarP)
updates.summary: Harmonized clinical trial protocol template. Mandates standardized protocol sections, structured data elements, and electronic exchange format. Enables digital protocols and automated regulatory processing.
impact_on_protocols: Protocol structure must follow M11 standardized sections
All protocols should be prepared in M11-compliant electronic format
Section numbering and content requirements are now harmonized globally

updates.title: Diversity Plans to Improve Enrollment of Participants from Underrepresented Populations
updates.summary: FDA now requires diversity action plans for Phase 3 and certain pivotal Phase 2 trials. Sponsors must set demographic enrollment targets and describe strategies to improve representation.
impact_on_protocols: Phase 3 protocols MUST include a diversity enrollment plan
Inclusion/exclusion criteria should be reviewed for unnecessary restrictions on diverse populations
Site selection should prioritize diverse patient populations
Decentralized trial elements should be considered to reduce access barriers

updates.title: Decentralized Clinical Trials for Drugs, Biological Products, and Devices
updates.summary: FDA final guidance on decentralized clinical trials (DCTs). Covers remote visits, eConsent, direct-to-patient drug delivery, telemedicine, and wearable devices in clinical trials.
impact_on_protocols: Protocols with any remote/decentralized elements must comply with DCT guidance
eConsent procedures must meet FDA electronic records requirements
Remote monitoring and telehealth visits need clear protocol procedures
Direct-to-patient drug delivery requires chain-of-custody documentation

updates.title: EU Health Technology Assessment Regulation (2021/2282)
updates.status: Regulation in force — Joint Clinical Assessments begin
updates.summary: EU-wide joint clinical assessments replace individual country HTA assessments for oncology drugs and ATMPs starting 2025, with full coverage by 2030. Requires early evidence generation planning aligned with EU HTA requirements.
impact_on_protocols: Oncology and ATMP protocols must plan for EU joint clinical assessment requirements
Comparator selection must align with EU HTA best available therapy
Patient-relevant endpoints required (EU HTA prefers clinical outcomes over surrogates)
Quality of life instruments must include EQ-5D-5L for EU HTA submissions

EMA.prime_designation_benefit: Enhanced early dialogue, accelerated assessment eligibility
